compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) is absent/bare soil. P3 (Pothos) is present with anchor. P4 (Silver Guest) is absent/bare soil. The 'New Introduction' is a recurring electronic component (IC chip) placed on the desk surface.

plant_audit.P3_Pothos: The specimen shows a single, persistent leaf. Posture is stable but static. No new growth observed over the 5-day period.
plant_audit.P1_P2_P4_Soil_Surface: All pots show dark, damp, undisturbed soil. No signs of germination or incidental growth.

biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of a small electronic component (IC chip) which has shifted position slightly between T-5 and Current. No fungal growth detected.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-4: Electronic component introduced to the desk surface.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_Current: Electronic component remains; no biological progression.

visual_health_inference.P3: Stagnant/Maintenance mode. The plant is alive but not thriving.
visual_health_inference.P1_P2_P4: Deceased/Lost. The lack of vegetative matter in the pots indicates total loss of the registered specimens.

anomalies: Introduction of an electronic IC chip on the desk surface.
Persistent absence of P1, P2, and P4 specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of arrested development. While the Pothos (P3) remains as a solitary survivor, the other registered plants (P1, P2, P4) have been lost, leaving only bare soil. The environment is static, with the only notable 'activity' being the placement and slight movement of an electronic component on the desk surface. The soil moisture levels appear high, yet there is no evidence of new biological activity or recovery.
